message:
fix(ft-nn): PReLU + 6 shrink/softmin/RReLU activations compose for autograd

Closes bead frankentorch-f6nn. Seven activation modules still read
tensor values, computed in plain f64, and rebuilt requires_grad=false
leaves — the same severed-autograd pattern that the SELU / Threshold /
Hardtanh / Unfold / Fold / CELU and the index_* / scatter_* /
masked_scatter fixes already cleaned up. PyTorch's nn variants of
all seven are fully differentiable.

Compositions (each through primitives that already have backward
kernels):

  Tanhshrink:  tensor_sub(input, tensor_tanh(input))
  Softsign:    tensor_div(input, 1 + tensor_abs(input))
  Softmin:     tensor_softmax(tensor_neg(input), dim)
  Hardshrink:  where(|x| > λ, x, 0)
  Softshrink:  where(x > λ, x - λ, where(x < -λ, x + λ, 0))
  RReLU:       where(x >= 0, x, midpoint_slope * x)
  PReLU:       where(x >= 0, x, weight_broadcast * x)
                where weight_broadcast is a tensor_reshape +
                tensor_expand chain that lifts the [num_parameters]
                weight to the full input shape — single-slope across
                all positions when num_parameters == 1, and
                per-channel along dim 1 when num_parameters ==
                channels.

Backward correctness falls out of the chain rule for each. PReLU now
also propagates gradient to its learnable weight (the previous body
dropped weight gradients entirely, which would have prevented
optimizer updates).

Tests (8 new gradient regression tests, ~one per activation):
  prelu_propagates_gradients_input_and_weight: single-slope, asserts
    grad_input piecewise (slope where x<0, 1 where x>=0) and
    grad_weight = sum of negative inputs.
  prelu_multichannel_propagates_gradients: 2-channel weight,
    [1, 2, 3] input, per-channel weight gradients verified
    analytically (channel 0: -3.0, channel 1: -4.0).
  softshrink_propagates_gradients: piecewise indicator
    [1, 0, 0, 0, 1] for the dead-zone vs shifted regions.
  hardshrink_propagates_gradients: same indicator pattern.
  tanhshrink_propagates_gradients: d/dx = tanh(x)^2 element-wise.
  softsign_propagates_gradients: d/dx = 1 / (1 + |x|)^2.
  softmin_propagates_gradients: sum-loss yields zero gradient
    everywhere (softmax sums to 1) — main contract is that the
    gradient *exists* at all (previous severed-tape returned None).
  rrelu_propagates_gradients: piecewise [slope where x<0, 1 where
    x>=0] with default lower/upper.

All 11 existing forward tests for these modules continue to pass —
bit-for-bit forward parity preserved.

Workspace: 516 ft-nn tests green (508 + 8 new), clippy clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
